author: Clement of Rome
book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.

classification.cautionReason: Valued early Christian text, but not part of the biblical canon for mainstream Christian denominations.

lineage: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.

ccel.title: ANF09. The Gospel of Peter, The Diatessaron of Tatian, The Apocalypse of Peter, the Vision of Paul, The Apocalypse of the Virgin and Sedrach, The Testament of Abraham, The Acts of Xanthippe and Polyxena, The Narrative of Zosimus, The Apology of Aristides, The Epistles of Clement (complete text), Origen’s Commentary on John, Books 1–10, and Commentary on Matthew, Books 1, 2, and 10–14.

source.title: ANF09. The Gospel of Peter, The Diatessaron of Tatian, The Apocalypse of Peter, the Vision of Paul, The Apocalypse of the Virgin and Sedrach, The Testament of Abraham, The Acts of Xanthippe and Polyxena, The Narrative of Zosimus, The Apology of Aristides, The Epistles of Clement (complete text), Origen’s Commentary on John, Books 1–10, and Commentary on Matthew, Books 1, 2, and 10–14.

title: The Praise of Love.

source.title: ANF09. The Gospel of Peter, The Diatessaron of Tatian, The Apocalypse of Peter, the Vision of Paul, The Apocalypse of the Virgin and Sedrach, The Testament of Abraham, The Acts of Xanthippe and Polyxena, The Narrative of Zosimus, The Apology of Aristides, The Epistles of Clement (complete text), Origen’s Commentary on John, Books 1–10, and Commentary on Matthew, Books 1, 2, and 10–14.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Let him who has love in Christ keep the commandments of Christ.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Who can describe the [blessed] bond of the love of God?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: What man is able to tell the excellence of its beauty, as it ought to be told?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: The height to which love exalts is unspeakable.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Love covers a multitude of sins.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Love beareth all things, is long-suffering in all things.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: There is nothing base, nothing arrogant in love.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Love admits of no schisms: love gives rise to no seditions: love does all things in harmony.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: By love have all the elect of God been made perfect; without love nothing is well-pleasing to God.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: In love has the Lord taken us to Himself.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: On account of the love He bore us, Jesus Christ our Lord gave His blood for us by the will of God; His flesh for our flesh, and His soul for our souls.
